channel.title: security on Have an average day :|
channel.description: Recent content in security on Have an average day :|

item.title: 7 of the best anti-virus apps for Linux
item.description: While ClamAV wasn’t the over all winner of this, and that is kind of surprising It didn’t come in last though. Given that they just used defaults in testing it scored 8/10. I’m pretty confident it could score 10/10 with some tweaking. I’ve always used Clam for quick cleanups of file shares and providing security on the Ubuntu boxes I deploy. Avast has always been my go to security software on Mac and Windows (when MSE & Malwarebytes aren’t an option).

item.title: 11 Offensive Security Tools for Wreaking Havoc | HackerTarget.com
item.description: 11 Offensive Security Tools for Wreaking Havoc via 11 Offensive Security Tools for Wreaking Havoc | HackerTarget.com.

item.title: How to mail enable an Active Directory Group on Exchange 2010
item.description: I needed to take a security group and convert it turn it into a distribution group. This is actually pretty simple. First edit the group and change it to a distribution group. Make sure it’s a universal group, only universal groups can be mail enabled. Give it an email address, make sure the membership is correct and move on to your exchange console. Go to distribution Groups Right Click New distribution Group Existing Account Done Now you have mail enabled your group.

item.title: Google to strip Chrome of SSL revocation checking
item.description: Google to strip Chrome of SSL revocation checking. At first I was appalled, but after reading this I can understand the point. I don’t like it though and think it’s a bad idea. Unless something better can be put in then I would rather have a ‘broken seat belt.’

item.title: Law Would Put Homeland Security in Charge of Business IT Security | CIO Blogs
item.description: Law Would Put Homeland Security in Charge of Business IT Security | CIO Blogs. This makes me kind of sick….

item.title: Odd Bits: Converting OpenSSH public keys
item.description: This is an interesting read. Odd Bits: Converting OpenSSH public keys. Converting OpenSSH public keys For reasons best left to another post, I wanted to convert an SSH public key into a PKCS# 1 PEM-encoded public key. That is, I wanted to go from this: ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAABAQD7EZn/BzP26AWk/Ts2ymjpTXuXRiEWIWn HFTilOTcuJ/P1HfOwiy4RHC1rv59Yh/E6jbTx623+OGySJWh1IS3dAEaHhcGKnJaikrBn3c cdoNVkAAuL/YD7FMG1Z0SjtcZS6MoO8Lb9pkq6R+Ok6JQjwCEsB+OaVwP9RnVA+HSYeyCVE 0KakLCbBJcD1U2aHP4+IH4OaXhZacpb9Ueja6NNfGrv558xTgfZ+fLdJ7cpg6wU8UZnVM1B JiUW5KFasc+2IuZR0+g/oJXaYwvW2T6XsMgipetCEtQoMAJ4zmugzHSQuFRYHw/7S6PUI2U 03glFmULvEV+qIxsVFT1ng3pj lars@tiamat.house To this: —–BEGIN RSA PUBLIC KEY—– MIIBCgKCAQEA+xGZ/wcz9ugFpP07Nspo6U17l0YhFiFpxxU4pTk3Lifz9R3zsIsu ERwta7+fWIfxOo208ett/jhskiVodSEt3QBGh4XBipyWopKwZ93HHaDVZAALi/2A +xTBtWdEo7XGUujKDvC2/aZKukfjpOiUI8AhLAfjmlcD/UZ1QPh0mHsglRNCmpCw mwSXA9VNmhz+PiB+Dml4WWnKW/VHo2ujTXxq7+efMU4H2fny3Se3KYOsFPFGZ1TN QSYlFuShWrHPtiLmUdPoP6CV2mML1tk+l7DIIqXrQhLUKDACeM5roMx0kLhUWB8P +0uj1CNlNN4JRZlC7xFfqiMbFRU9Z4N6YwIDAQAB —–END RSA PUBLIC KEY—– If you have a recent version of OpenSSH (where recent means 5.
